name: Writing Great Skills
description_en: A reference of the vocabulary and principles that make an agent skill predictable, for writing and editing skills well.
description_zh: 一份关于词汇与原则的参考，讲解如何让智能体技能更可预测，用于把技能写好、改好。
long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/writing-great-skills folder, not the whole repo). It is a meta-skill: a pure reference that defines the levers behind predictability — invocation modes, description writing, the information hierarchy and progressive disclosure, when to split skills, pruning to a single source of truth, and using leading words — plus a catalog of steering failure modes (including Negation, where naming what not to do backfires, and Negative Space, the steering done by what a skill leaves out), with a bundled GLOSSARY.md for the full definitions.
long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/writing-great-skills 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它是一个元技能：一份纯参考资料，阐明决定“可预测性”的各项杠杆——调用方式、描述撰写、信息层级与渐进式披露、何时拆分技能、剪枝至单一事实来源，以及善用“引导词”——外加一份“引导失败模式”清单（包括 Negation：写明“不要做什么”反而弄巧成拙；以及 Negative Space：技能所省略之处同样在引导行为），并附带一份 GLOSSARY.md 提供完整定义。
sec_note_en: Pure reference material consulted on demand: it makes no external changes (no files written, no commands run, no network access). It only shapes how you author other skills.
sec_note_zh: 按需查阅的纯参考资料：它不做任何外部改动（不写文件、不执行命令、不联网）。它只影响你如何撰写其他技能。
